title: Fuze A2A profile (v1) — constraints layered on the open A2A AgentCard
description: A card MUST validate against BOTH agent-card.schema.json and this profile. The profile narrows the open standard to the subset the Fuze family freezes for v1. Narrowing only — it never contradicts A2A, so a Fuze card remains consumable by any conformant third-party A2A client.

supportedInterfaces.description: v1 freezes a SINGLE interface: JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP with SSE streaming, A2A 1.0. gRPC and HTTP+JSON are OUT of v1 (see binding.md).

capabilities.description: v1 baseline: streaming REQUIRED true (the Managed-Agents driver already holds an SSE stream, so this is free); pushNotifications REQUIRED false in v1 (no webhook egress path is frozen yet); extendedAgentCard REQUIRED true (skill visibility is caller-dependent — see authz.md).

signatures.description: REQUIRED and non-empty in the Fuze profile. A2A leaves signing optional, but the callee-enforced authZ model (authz.md) treats the card as the published capability boundary — an unsigned card cannot be trusted to state which skills a caller may reach.
